recentArticles.title: What Jay-Z has in his watch collection, from one of the most complicated designs ever made to a $6 million timepiece - Business Insider
recentArticles.snippet: # What Jay-Z has in his watch collection, from one of the most complicated designs ever made to a $6 million timepiece. Jay-Z once rapped the line "Time is money." When he said that in his film "Streets Is Watching," he meant it quite literally. You might remember the $1,075,652 Patek Philippe Grand Complication 5304-301R he wore at the 2025 Grammys. That was only one example — and it's far from the most expensive in his collection. ## Jay-Z has a Patek Philippe Grand Complication 5304-301R that retails for more than $1 million. Jay-Z, born Shawn Carter, is a big fan of Patek Philippe watches. He wore one with a rose-gold case and dozens of diamonds for the 2025 Grammys. On the Patek Philippe website, the watch is valued at $1,075,652. In 2012, his wife and fellow artist Beyoncé gifted Jay-Z a Hublot Big Bang in 18-karat white gold for his 43rd birthday.
recentArticles.summary: What Jay-Z has in his watch collection, from one of the most complicated designs ever made to a $6 million timepiece - Business Insider

recentArticles.title: Jay-Z, Drake, Justin Bieber & Don Toliver Soundtrack Official 2026 Fanatics Fest Playlist - Billboard
recentArticles.snippet: # Jay-Z, Drake, Justin Bieber & Don Toliver Soundtrack Official 2026 Fanatics Fest Playlist. Fanatics Fest is returning to NYC this weekend, and Audiomack unveiled the official playlist to soundtrack the festivities on Tuesday (July 14). The 50-track playlist boasts hits like Drake’s “Shabang,” Jay-Z’s “Empire State of Mind,” Justin Bieber’s “Yukon,” Sabrina Carpenter’s “Bed Chem,” Pop Smoke’s “Dior,” Don Toliver’s “E85” and Taylor Swift’s “Anti-Hero.”. “At Audiomack, we believe that discovering new artists is one of the most exciting parts of being a music fan. Fanatics Fest brings together sports, music, and entertainment in a way that feels incredibly authentic, so becoming the official streaming partner was a perfect fit. We’re excited to help soundtrack the weekend while introducing fans to the next generation of artists,” said Audiomack’s co-founder and chief marketing officer, **David Ponte**. Sports and music have been synonymous for decades, and those worlds will clash once again at Fanatics Fest, which takes over the Javits Center in NYC from July 16 through July 19.
recentArticles.summary: Jay-Z, Drake, Justin Bieber & Don Toliver Soundtrack Official 2026 Fanatics Fest Playlist - Billboard

recentArticles.title: Jay-Z Closes Yankee Stadium Residency With Beyoncé, Rihanna and Usher - Variety
recentArticles.snippet: # Jay-Z Closes Yankee Stadium Residency With Epic Late Night Show Featuring Beyoncé, Rihanna, Usher, Teyana Taylor and More. “We’re going to go until like 5 a.m., so don’t be scared,” said Jay-Z not long after he started the third and final show of his Yankee Stadium residency on Sunday night, the concluding chapter to a weekend that paid homage to the 25th and 30th anniversaries of his seminal albums “The Blueprint” and “Reasonable Doubt,” respectively. The remark was made around 1 a.m, roughly 40 minutes after he started a concert that had been plagued by hours-long delays as fans waited both inside the stadium and on the perimeter of the venue, stuck in lines that didn’t budge. It was a ceaseless tour of his discography accented and enhanced by a rotating cast of guest artists that, to do justice, is best laid out as a plain list: Beyoncé, Rihanna, Usher, Jermaine Dupri, Pharrell Williams, Clipse, Teyana Taylor, Swizz Beatz, Jeezy, Fat Joe, Jadakiss and The-Dream.
recentArticles.summary: Jay-Z Closes Yankee Stadium Residency With Beyoncé, Rihanna and Usher - Variety

recentArticles.title: ‘They say I sold out’: Jay-Z challenges Target partnership critics in Yankee Stadium freestyle - Yahoo
recentArticles.snippet: # ‘They say I sold out’: Jay-Z challenges Target partnership critics in Yankee Stadium freestyle. Jay-Z is finally responding to critics who questioned his decision to partner with Target amid the retailer's ongoing boycott over its rollback of diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives. During opening night of his three-night Yankee Stadium residency celebrating the 30th anniversary of "Reasonable Doubt," the Brooklyn rap icon used a newly minted freestyle to address the controversy head-on, dismissing accusations that he abandoned Black consumers by releasing a Target-exclusive vinyl edition of his landmark debut album. It was Jay-Z choosing one of the biggest stages of his career to publicly answer a question that has followed him for months: whether one of hip-hop's first billionaire artists can balance corporate partnerships with the expectations of a community that increasingly views consumer spending as a form of political action.
recentArticles.summary: ‘They say I sold out’: Jay-Z challenges Target partnership critics in Yankee Stadium freestyle - Yahoo

recentArticles.title: Jay-Z’s Yankee Stadium Concert Delayed For Hours After Alleged Security Breach And Lockdown - Forbes
recentArticles.snippet: # Jay-Z’s Yankee Stadium Concert Delayed For Hours After Alleged Security Breach And Lockdown. Jay-Z’s concert at Yankee Stadium in New York was delayed by several hours on Sunday night following a reported security breach that led to a lockdown, prompting the billionaire rapper to take the stage after midnight and apologize to fans, saying he was concerned about people getting trampled. Sunday’s event was the last of Jay-Z’s three nights of concerts in New York and its start was delayed by more than four hours, according to local news outlet ABC7 Eyewitness News. The rapper eventually took the stage after midnight as security allowed fans to trickle in and apologized for the delay. Explaining the delay, Jay-Z said there were “like 10,000 people outside” when the doors were shut and he didn’t want to start before everyone entered to avoid a stampede-like situation. According to Forbes’ estimates, Jay-Z has a net worth of $2.8 billion as of Monday morning.
recentArticles.summary: Jay-Z’s Yankee Stadium Concert Delayed For Hours After Alleged Security Breach And Lockdown - Forbes

recentArticles.title: Jay-Z's final Yankee Stadium show was a trip down memory lane. See the surprise celebrity guests he brought onstage. - Business Insider
recentArticles.snippet: On Sunday, New York City's Yankee Stadium was certainly a concrete jungle where dreams are made of — at least, for the thousands of Jay-Z fans who waited hours to see the rapper take the stage for the third and final night of his residency there. A four-hour delay caused the show to kick off around 12:15 a.m., instead of the scheduled 8 p.m. In a joint statement, Roc Nation and Yankee Stadium told Business Insider that "hundreds of individuals in large groups — who did not have tickets to tonight's performance — stormed over peaceful ticketholders, and in some cases, breached security," which led to the gates being closed for "an extended period of time.". For many, it was worth the wait, as friends, family, and longtime collaborators joined one of New York City's most beloved musicians onstage, including Beyoncé, Rihanna, Usher, Pharrell Williams, Jeezy, Fat Joe, and more.
recentArticles.summary: Jay-Z's final Yankee Stadium show was a trip down memory lane. See the surprise celebrity guests he brought onstage. - Business Insider

recentArticles.title: Jay-Z apologizes after Yankee Stadium show starts hours late, says there was a safety issue - NBC News
recentArticles.snippet: # Jay-Z apologizes after Yankee Stadium show starts hours late, says there was a safety issue. Sunday night’s show wrapped Jay-Z’s three-night hometown concert at the stadium to celebrate the 30th anniversary of his debut album, “Reasonable Doubt.”. Jay-Z apologized to fans after an hourslong delay at his Sunday night show, telling the crowd at Yankee Stadium that a safety issue prompted security to close the doors. It was like 10,000 people outside and they closed all the doors. Somebody rushed the door, and they closed the door for you guys’ safety and everybody’s safety outside,” he said in a video posted on X. Sunday night’s show wrapped Jay-Z’s three-night hometown concert at the stadium to celebrate the 30th anniversary of his debut album — “Reasonable Doubt” — and the 25th anniversary of “The Blueprint.” Special guests at the shows included his wife and singer-songwriter Beyoncé, Alicia Keys, Nas, Eminem, Rihanna, Usher, Teyana Taylor and Pharrell Williams, according to the Hollywood Reporter.
recentArticles.summary: Jay-Z apologizes after Yankee Stadium show starts hours late, says there was a safety issue - NBC News

recentArticles.snippet: * Click here to listen to this article. This is read by an automated voice. See more from the L.A. Times in Google Search. Jay-Z brought a parade of guests to the stage Sunday night at New York’s Yankee Stadium, where the veteran rapper closed a three-night stand marking the 30th anniversary of his debut album, “Reasonable Doubt,” and the 25th anniversary of his commercial breakthrough, “The Blueprint.”. Rihanna joined Jay-Z for “Run This Town,” then stuck around to do her “Bitch Better Have My Money,” according to reports in Variety and the New York Times. Variety said the show ended at 3 a.m. Sunday’s show followed two earlier Yankee Stadium gigs in which Jay-Z brought out Beyoncé and their daughter, Blue Ivy Carter, as well as Nas, Eminem, Memphis Bleek, Jaz-O and Slick Rick. In September, Jay-Z is scheduled to perform stadium concerts in London and Paris ahead of an Oct. 23 date at Inglewood’s SoFi Stadium. ### More to Read.

recentArticles.title: Why Michael Jackson’s daughter, Paris, won’t stop ‘til she gets enough from his estate - Yahoo
recentArticles.snippet: She is asking a court to take a closer look at how the pop icon's estate is being handled by its executors – the people responsible for managing it. Paris Jackson has accused executors John Branca and John McClain of paying themselves and the estate's lawyers too much, and for leaving $464 million owned by the estate uninvested. Paris Jackson also has accused Branca of misusing his position as producer of "Michael," an upcoming Michael Jackson biopic reportedly financed by Jackson's estate, to cast an A-list celebrity – Miles Teller – to play the role of Branca himself in the film. As law professors who study the transfer of property after death, we find that when disputes over inherited wealth become national news, they are often difficult to understand because this type of legal process is obscure and most people never interact directly with the probate court system.
recentArticles.summary: Why Michael Jackson’s daughter, Paris, won’t stop ‘til she gets enough from his estate - Yahoo

recentArticles.title: Jay-Z Celebrates ‘The Blueprint’ 25th Anniversary With Eminem, Pharrell Williams and Slick Rick at Yankee Stadium - Variety Australia
recentArticles.snippet: # Jay-Z Celebrates ‘The Blueprint’ 25th Anniversary With Eminem, Pharrell Williams and Slick Rick at Yankee Stadium. Jay-Z was almost entirely truthful to the source material for his 25th anniversary Yankee Stadium show on Saturday to celebrate “The Blueprint,” setting guests artists to high wattage but low frequency as he trotted out Eminem, Pharrell Williams and Slick Rick for a curtailed 90-minute set. The show began with instant homage as Jay brought out Slick Rick for “The Ruler’s Back,” launching into the factory line of songs that made up the spine of “The Blueprint.” There were stadium-sized highs (“U Don’t Know”), somber lows (“Song Cry”) and a highly assumed appearance from Eminem, who traded verses with Jay on “Renegade” and reemerged to perform his signature hit “Lose Yourself.”. Jay then kicked back up with “Empire State of Mind,” New York City’s torch song that he performed on Friday with Alicia Keys, before enlisting Pharrell Williams for a slew of collaborative hits spanning “I Just Wanna Love U (Give It 2 Me)” to “Frontin.” The show then, surprisingly, finished quickly, with “N—s in Paris,” “Public Service Announcement” and “Encore.”.
recentArticles.summary: Jay-Z Celebrates ‘The Blueprint’ 25th Anniversary With Eminem, Pharrell Williams and Slick Rick at Yankee Stadium - Variety Australia

recentArticles.title: Security breach causes chaos, delays start of final Jay-Z concert at Yankee Stadium - ABC7 New York
recentArticles.snippet: # Security breach causes chaos, delays start of final Jay-Z concert at Yankee Stadium. THE BRONX (WABC) -- There was chaos at Jay-Z's third and final Yankee Stadium concert after a security breach delayed the show. Thousands of fans were seen outside the stadium waiting to get inside. A police source tells Eyewitness News a large group broke through security, prompting a full lockdown of the stadium. For a while, no one was allowed in or out. "We got here a little after 8, went to Gate 2, they bum rushed Gate 2. People went past security, were not checked and got into the stadium. They closed down Gate 2, we went to Gate 4, same thing happened," said Rosalynn Glover, "We were at the suite section, they would not even let us in there. The people working at Gate 4 were laughing. The concert was scheduled to begin at 8 p.m. The gates reopened shortly before 10 p.m. with police patrolling each entrance.
recentArticles.summary: Security breach causes chaos, delays start of final Jay-Z concert at Yankee Stadium - ABC7 New York

recentArticles.title: Jay-Z Reasonable Doubt at 30: Beyoncé & Nas at Yankee Stadium - Lower Bucks Times
recentArticles.snippet: HomeEntertainmentJay-Z Brought Out Beyoncé, Blue Ivy and Nas for Reasonable Doubt at... # Jay-Z Brought Out Beyoncé, Blue Ivy and Nas for Reasonable Doubt at 30, and Yankee Stadium Lost Its Mind. Jay-Z’s Reasonable Doubt 30th anniversary concert at Yankee Stadium was billed as a victory lap for one man, and it turned into the biggest family reunion hip-hop has staged in years. On Friday, July 10, the rapper born Shawn Carter walked out into the Bronx ballpark to play his 1996 debut front to back, and by the end of the night he had shared the stage with his wife, his oldest daughter, his fiercest one-time rival and one of the most famous voices in New York music. It was the first of three sold-out shows, and it set a bar that will be hard to top. On this night, roughly 45,000 people paid to hear every word of it, and Jay-Z spent the evening reminding them how far that record traveled.
recentArticles.summary: Jay-Z Reasonable Doubt at 30: Beyoncé & Nas at Yankee Stadium - Lower Bucks Times

recentArticles.title: Jay-Z Brings Out Beyoncé, Blue Ivy Carter at Yankee Stadium - Variety
recentArticles.snippet: # Jay-Z Brings Out Beyoncé, Blue Ivy Carter, Nas, Alicia Keys and More for ‘Reasonable Doubt’ 30th Anniversary Show at Yankee Stadium. That didn’t stop him from pulling out all the stops — well, most of them, at least — during the first of his three headlining shows at Yankee Stadium on Friday night, setting off a string of performances to commemorate a pair of milestones (the other being the 25th anniversary of “The Blueprint”) plus a bonus show billed as “Extra Innings” that, as of now, has a stadium-sized question mark hanging over what it will actually be. For his “Reasonable Doubt” show, it wasn’t entirely off-script — every song from the record was performed in one way or another — yet he turned the night into a Jay-Z history lesson, weaving in bits and bobs from other hits and bringing out a murderer’s row of guests spanning his family (Beyoncé, Blue Ivy Carter) to friends and former foes (Nas, Jaz-O) in an effort to round out the world he’d built three decades ago.
recentArticles.summary: Jay-Z Brings Out Beyoncé, Blue Ivy Carter at Yankee Stadium - Variety

recentArticles.title: Paris Jackson on the Perfume Oil That Reminds Her of Her Father - Vogue
recentArticles.snippet: # Paris Jackson on the Saffron Scent That Reminds Her of Her Father. Paris Jackson is truly showing us her daily routine: get dressed, do some light glam, go to the studio, work on her music. This is truly a moment to celebrate for the singer because there was a time when she was scared of doing music. “In my early 20s, I realized I couldn’t do this anymore—it’s what makes me happy...especially because this was before I found out all the other things that made me happy. The musician starts with her hair: She doesn’t wash it every day, but has a several-step routine that’s focused on growth and strength. “I buzzed my hair just over 10 years ago, and I realized in the last few years that constantly bleaching and straightening it was not helping it get longer,” she says with a shrug. “I started taking care of my skin a few years ago,” Jackson says, “I was just doing it wrong.
recentArticles.summary: Paris Jackson on the Perfume Oil That Reminds Her of Her Father - Vogue

recentArticles.title: Jay-Z returns to Yankee Stadium with three-show celebration - NBC New York
recentArticles.snippet: New York City 2 hours ago. # Jay-Z returns to Yankee Stadium with three-show celebration. Jay-Z is taking over Yankee Stadium this weekend with a three-night concert event celebrating major milestones in his career: 30 years since the release of his album “Reasonable Doubt” and 25 years since “The Blueprint.” Fans from across the region are lining up at pop-ups and for the highly anticipated performances. NBC New York’s Kay Angrum reports. #### Inside ‘The Last Twins,' a new documentary about a Holocaust survivor and hero. recentArticles.snippet: * Book Club with Mary Calvi. * Class Act with Chris Wragge. * The 5:33 | Legacy of the LIRR Massacre. * On The Dot with David Schechter: Wildfires & Climate Change. * Climate Change: Protecting our Planet. # Jay-Z plays to sold-out crowd at Yankee Stadium for anniversary concert series. latestReport.daily_summary: 周杰倫近期話題不斷，鳥巢演唱會前夕，他攜小女兒抵京，兒子展現獨立。然而，杭州演唱會卻引發「划水」質疑。儘管如此，林志穎帶兒子觀看演唱會的溫馨互動獲讚。國台辦也對周杰倫等台灣歌手在大陸辦演唱會做出回應，顯示其影響力。
